cards.title: Supporting Your Child's Academic Journey Without Tutors
cards.body: You may be projecting your own school experiences onto your daughter—her needs are different. Focus on providing emotional support and confidence rather than expensive external help.

cards.title: Managing Parental Anxiety During Family Holidays
cards.body: Excessive worry can rob you and your children of enjoying family time together. Learning to cope with anxiety during real emergencies shows kids resilience is possible.

cards.title: Breaking Dysfunctional Family Patterns in Relationships
cards.body: Children who experienced narcissistic or avoidant parenting often struggle to recognize healthy relationships as adults. Therapy and self-awareness are key to breaking harmful cycles.

cards.title: Only Children Are Not Weirdos or Lonely
cards.body: Stereotypes about only children being selfish or maladjusted persist despite evidence to the contrary. Parents choosing to have one child deserve support, not judgment.

cards.title: The Real Reason Behind Kids' Attention-Seeking Behavior
cards.body: When children constantly demand attention, firmer boundaries rarely solve the problem. Understanding the root cause of their needs is far more effective than punishment.

cards.title: Raising Decision-Makers in a High-Stakes World
cards.body: Brain development continues until age 25, so teens need guidance, not control. Teach kids decision-making skills by letting them make age-appropriate choices with consequences.

cards.title: Verbal Abuse From Parents Affects Adult Children
cards.body: Interactions with a parent that leave you doubting yourself or feeling guilty may indicate emotional abuse. Reclaiming your voice and setting boundaries is essential for healing.

cards.title: NHS Launches Largest Maternity Review in History
cards.body: The review of Nottingham University Hospitals will detail how systemic failures led to preventable deaths and harm. This comprehensive investigation aims to protect future mothers and babies.
